@@ -0,0 +1,84 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "linkerd-13169-linkerd-tap-doesn-t-seem-to-work-with-eks-access-entries-authentic",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "linkerd: Linkerd Tap doesn't seem to work with EKS Access Entries authentication",
+    "description": "Linkerd Tap doesn't seem to work with EKS Access Entries authentication. This issue affects 6+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify linkerd troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your linkerd deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd -n linkerd --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review linkerd configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant linkerd configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd
kubectl get configmap -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=linkerd
```
### What is the issue?

I was trying to use `linkerd viz tap` to debug an internal issue, but kept getting `tap authorization failed`."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Linkerd Tap doesn't seem to work with EKS Access Entries…",
+        "description": "We have this same issue.

```
Kubernetes Version: v1.31
Cluster Environment: AWS EKS
Host OS: Amazon Bottlerocket
linkerd version
Client version: edge-25.3.2
Server version: edge-25.1.2
```

We can not tap in the UI, or on the CLI

```

yes
```

```
 linkerd viz tap -n linkerd
```yaml
linkerd-identity
----------------
× trust anchors are using supported crypto algorithm
    Invalid trustAnchors:
        * 498364598153846236906649535816328601770266843336 One Model Linkerd Root CA must use P-256 curve for public key, instead P-384 was used
    see https://linkerd.io/2/checks/#l5d-identity-trustAnchors-use-supported-crypto for hints

linkerd-jaeger
--------------
‼ jaeger extension proxies are up-to-date
    some proxies are not running the current version:
        * collector-9cb9f47cb-2p4dn (edge-24.10.1)
        * collector-9cb9f47cb-s8zg2 (edge-24.10.1)
        * jaeger-in
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade linkerd to include the fix",
+        "description": "If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade linkerd:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade linkerd linkerd/linkerd --namespace linkerd
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n linkerd
helm list -n linkerd
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm Linkerd Tap doesn't seem to work with EKS Access… is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd -n linkerd --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n linkerd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "We have this same issue.

```
Kubernetes Version: v1.31
Cluster Environment: AWS EKS
Host OS: Amazon Bottlerocket
linkerd version
Client version: edge-25.3.2
Server version: edge-25.1.2
```

We can not tap in the UI, or on the CLI

```

yes
```

```
 linkerd viz tap -n linkerd deploy/linkerd-controller --as $(whoami)
Cannot connect to Linkerd Viz: namespaces is forbidden: User "justin" cannot",
